iti69:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti69:2.1: “Monks, anyone–monk or nun–in whom passion is unabandoned, aversion is unabandoned, & delusion is unabandoned,
iti69:2.2: is said not to have crossed the ocean with its waves, breakers, & whirlpools, its seizers & demons.
iti69:2.3: Anyone–monk or nun–in whom passion is abandoned, aversion is abandoned, & delusion is abandoned,
iti69:2.4:
is said to have crossed the ocean with its waves, breakers, & whirlpools, its seizers & demons.
Having crossed over, having reached the far shore, he/she stands on high ground, a brahman.”
iti69:3.3: has crossed over this ocean with its seizers, demons,
iti69:3.4: dangerous waves, so hard to cross.
iti69:4.1: Free from acquisitions –bonds surmounted, death abandoned–
iti69:4.2: he has abandoned stress with no further becoming.
iti69:4.3: Having gone to the goal he is undefined,
iti69:4.4: has outwitted, I tell you, the King of Death.
